page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.medium is an ARM64-based compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated AWS Graviton processor using the Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture. It features 1 physical core with a single vCPU running at 2.5 GHz, backed by 64 KB L1D cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory and includes a bundled 59 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed scratch storage. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the c6gd.medium demonstrates moderate single-core capabilities but limited multi-core scaling due to its single-vCPU configuration. Stress-ng single-core performance is rated as average at 1490.59, while multi-core performance matches this figure, placing it in the lower tier for multi-threaded workloads. Memory bandwidth tests show 59.39 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 20.1 GB/sec for large-block reads and 9.8 GB/sec for large-block writes. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as single-threaded gzip compression at 19.76 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 7.42 GB/sec, reflect weak to poor performance profiles.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c6gd.medium offers a low-cost entry point into the ARM64 ecosystem with dedicated compute resources and local NVMe storage, avoiding the CPU credit limitations of burstable instances. However, the single-core constraint and 2.0 GB memory limit represent significant tradeoffs for resource-intensive applications. This instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic static web serving, simple database operations, and development or testing environments that benefit from local SSD storage but do not require high network bandwidth or multi-threaded processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.medium is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance powered by a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 core running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 2.0 GB of memory and a bundled 59 GB local NVMe SSD. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1490.59, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are low due to the single-vCPU limitation. The instance achieves 1.19 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 1.14 GB/sec in static web serving. Qualitatively, it provides a cost-effective, non-burstable entry point for ARM64 development, avoiding CPU credit limits. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and local-storage-dependent testing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.medium is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton processor, 2.0 GB of memory, and a 59 GB local NVMe SSD, running on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.medium is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 1 dedicated vCPU, 2.0 GB of memory, and a 59 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: A dedicated single-core ARM64 compute instance featuring integrated local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight, non-burstable workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton ARM64 processor
Includes 2.0 GB of system memory and a 59 GB local NVMe SSD
Delivers average single-core performance but limited multi-threaded capacity
Runs on the lightweight AWS Nitro hypervisor architecture
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and static web serving
